Title: Trump says he's raising new global tariff rate to 15% following Supreme Court loss
Description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said on social media he is raising the global tariff from 10 per cent to 15 per cent, though these temporary tariffs can only be imposed for 150 days unless Congress approves an extension. The move follows an executive order he signed Friday imposing a 10 per cent tariff on imports from all countries after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down tariffs he introduced last year.

Title: Transport Canada certifies Gulfstream jets amid Trump aviation tariff threat
Description:
Transport Canada has certified the American-made Gulfstream G500 and G600 business jets, according to a document posted on the federal governmentвАЩs website. These jets were at the centre of U.S. President Donald TrumpвАЩs aviation dispute last month, accusing Canada of 'illegally' refusing to certify several Gulfstream models. Trump threatened to impose a 50 per cent tariff on Canadian aircraft, including Bombardier jets, and said his administration would 'decertify' them. He has not followed through on his threat.

Title: Mar-a-lago shooting update: Trump not at Palm Beach residence at time of Secret Service shooting
Description:
U.S. Secret Service agents and local police shot and killed an armed man who allegedly breached a secure perimeter at Mar-a-Lago and raised a gun at law enforcement officials in the early hours of Sunday, Feb. 22.

A security detail detected the man near the north gate of President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort in Palm Beach, Florida, at about 1:30 a.m. local time. Authorities said he was carrying a shotgun and a fuel can. The president was in Washington during the incident and not at his property in Palm Beach, Florida.

Title: NYSE after Supreme Court rules against President Trump's tariffs
Description:
The Supreme Court dealt a major blow to President Donald Trump's economic agenda, ruling that he does not have the authority to impose sweeping tariffs at the stroke of a pen.

The court on Feb. 20 tossed the tariffs that are the centerpiece of his economic policy and a major foreign policy tool вАУ but that have also raised costs for consumers and businesses. It is the first major ruling against Trump's controversial expansive view of presidential power.

Title: Trump denounces Supreme Court justices after tariff decision
Description:
President Donald Trump on Feb. 20 denounced Supreme Court justices who ruled against him as a вАЬdisgraceвАЭ and announced wide-ranging new tariffs hours after the high court struck down most of his existing import taxes. In a 6-3 decision, the justices had ruled the president did not have the authority under a 1977 emergency economic powers law to impose import levies on goods from nearly all of the nationвАЩs trading partners. 

In response, Trump used a different law to impose a 10 percent across-the-board tariff. Read more: https://wapo.st/4b1GSfC. Subscribe to The Washington Post on YouTube: https://wapo.st/2QOdcqK

Title: New York leaders rehang Stonewall Pride flag, defying Trump administration
Description:
New York City leaders rehung the Pride flag in front of the Stonewall National Monument on Feb. 12, a rebuke of the Trump administrationвАЩs efforts to take down the symbol of the LGBTQ+ rights movement from federal property.

The Trump administration had previously removed the Pride flag and replaced it with the American flag, outraging many New Yorkers. The National Park Service said the Pride flag was taken down to comply with an Interior Department order limiting what types of flags and logos can be displayed on federal property.

Title: Armed man killed after entering secure perimeter of Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence | BBC News
Description:
An armed man has been shot dead after entering the secure perimeter of US President Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence in Florida, the Secret Service has said.

The man was carrying a shotgun and fuel can when he was stopped and shot by Secret Service agents and a Sheriff's deputy, authorities said.

The incident happened around 01:30 ET (06:30 GMT) on Sunday morning, when the president was in Washington DC.

The suspect's identity has not been released yet but he was white and in his early 20s, officers said.

Title: Donald Trump to raise global tariffs to 15% after 'anti-American' Supreme Court verdict | BBC News
Description:
US President Donald Trump has said that he will impose global tariffs of 15%, as he continued to rail against a Supreme Court ruling that struck down his previous import taxes.
 
Trump said on Friday that he would replace the tariffs scrapped by the court with a 10% levy on all goods coming into the US.
 
But on Saturday, he announced on Truth Social that this would be increased to the maximum allowed under a never-used trade law.
 
These will come into force on Tuesday 24 February and can only stay in place for around five months before the administration must seek congressional approval.
 
Trump said his administration had reached the decision following a review of the "ridiculous, poorly written, and extraordinarily anti-American decision on Tariffs issued yesterday".

Title: US President Donald Trump 'considering' military strikes on Iran | BBC News
Description:
US President Donald Trump has said he is considering a limited military strike on Iran in order to pressure its leaders to agree a deal to curb the nuclear programme.
 
On Thursday, Trump said the world would find out "over the next, probably, 10 days" whether a deal would be reached or the US would take military action. The US has been increasing its military presence in the region in recent weeks.
 
The deployment includes the world's largest warship, USS Gerald R Ford, appearing to be heading towards the region.

Title: How close is Trump to attacking Iran? | BBC News
Description:
For weeks, Washington has been building up its forces in the Middle East - and now looks set to assemble more air power in the region than at any time since the 2003 invasion of Iraq. 
 
President Donald Trump has been pressuring the Iranian regime to make a nuclear deal, saying Tehran has a deadline of "10, 15 days" before it could face potential military action. He has told reporters he's considering "limited strikes". Iran has vowed to retaliate if the US launches any attacks.
 
Merlyn Thomas from BBC Verify has been tracking the buildup of ships and fighter jets in the Middle East, including confirming the location of the US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln - equipped with guided missile destroyers and dozens of fighter jets - near Iran. USS Gerald R Ford, the world's largest warship, has also been spotted, seemingly en route to the region. 
 
She explains the military fire power that the US could unleash on Iran, and how it compares with last year's Operation Hammer, when the US struck Iran's nuclear sites.

Title: Ghislaine Maxwell Refuses to Answer Epstein Questions From Congress
Description:

Maxwell, who appeared virtually from a prison in Texas, invoked her Fifth Amendment right against self-incrimination in response to every question asked. Her lawyer said in his opening statement that Maxwell вАЬwould answer questions if she were granted clemencyвАЭ by President Trump.
